limitations: This is a report-only promotion-contract control object.
It does not approve authoring, publication, claim insertion, or docs/book updates.
Future constrained_authoring_context_candidate transition is reserved for a later run.
The evidence remains narrow, singleton-derived, and caveat-only.
promotion_contract_candidates.residual_risk: Downstream stages could overpromote tooling-adjacency metadata into dependency or operating-environment language if later gates strip the mandatory caveat.

selected_metadata_preflights.caveat_integrity_assessment: Caveat integrity is adequate for a controlled metadata stage: the required narrow framing is present, repeated, and reinforced by negative boundaries against runtime-dependency, operating-environment, web-access, phone-access, and claim-insertion interpretations.
do_not_say: Do not say Hermes is a runtime dependency of OpenClaw.
Do not say Hermes is the general operating environment for OpenClaw.
Do not say OpenClaw requires Hermes for web or phone access.
Do not generalize beyond migration/setup/import tooling contexts unless additional sources support it.
Do not use this material as a factual claim without the caveat.
Do not use this material for chapter prose before later author/red-team gates pass.
selected_metadata_preflights.do_not_say_compliance_assessment: The metadata preserves all hard do-not-say constraints and does not authorize any forbidden inference; it explicitly blocks conversion of tooling adjacency into dependency, operating-environment, access, chapter-prose, or publication claims.
selected_metadata_preflights.evidence_use_assessment: Evidence use is correctly limited to narrow metadata-level factual atoms about OpenClaw documentation summaries naming Hermes in migration and setup tooling contexts, with explicit denial of broader dependency, runtime, access, or operating-environment support.
limitations: The material is caveat-only and advisory.
The metadata cannot be used as chapter prose, publishable wording, or claim insertion support.
The cited source identifiers and review artifacts must be checked again before any later factual use.
The canary was assessed as improved but still thin.
Target chapter placement remains suggested only, not assigned or approved.
selected_metadata_preflights.metadata_containment_assessment: The selected object is substantially contained as metadata: it consists of identifiers, caveats, allowed and forbidden intent constraints, provenance paths, evidence-bound atoms, blockers, and risk notes. The quoted canary text is present only as labeled report-only canary material and must not be promoted into prose.
promotion_blockers: No authoring approval has been granted.
No publication approval has been granted.
Later gates must prevent prose-promotion from tooling adjacency into dependency or operating-environment claims.
Any later use must preserve the mandatory caveat and re-check upstream source/review artifacts.
selected_metadata_preflights.provenance_assessment: Provenance is sufficient for a controlled metadata/promotion-contract stage because it retains source, note, manifest, cluster, packet, rebuilt input, canary, red-team, candidate source, and review identifiers, plus report paths; it is not sufficient as resolved citation support for authoring or publication.
required_caveats: Frame narrowly: OpenClaw documentation names Hermes in migration/setup tooling contexts; do not state that Hermes is a runtime dependency or general operating environment without additional sources.
selected_metadata_preflights.residual_risk: Low if retained as advisory caveat-only planning metadata; moderate if later review treats tooling adjacency as dependency; high if used to support runtime, access, or operating-environment claims.
selected_metadata_preflights.residual_risk_assessment: Residual risk is controlled at the metadata/preflight layer because the object repeatedly blocks authoring and unsupported inferences, but downstream prose-promotion risk remains material if a later stage strips the caveat or treats the canary wording as chapter-ready text.
unsupported_inferences: Hermes is a runtime dependency of OpenClaw.
Hermes is the general operating environment for OpenClaw.
OpenClaw requires Hermes for web or phone access.
Migration/setup/import tooling adjacency proves architectural dependency.
The canary is publishable or chapter-ready.
The canary can be inserted as a claim without later gates.
selected_metadata_preflights.usefulness_as_metadata_assessment: The metadata is useful for a constrained promotion-contract update because it preserves narrow evidence boundaries, provenance, caveats, blockers, and negative constraints; it remains too narrow for authoring, publication, or claim insertion.
